items.title: PM tells Ratcliffe to apologise for 'colonised' remarks
items.summary: Sir Keir Starmer says comments by the businessman and Manchester United co-owner are "offensive and wrong".

items.title: Sean Dyche sacked by Nottingham Forest after Wolves draw
items.summary: Evangelos Marinakis often takes action when the crowd turns against a head coach. The Nottingham Forest owner watched from his throne, enduring 90 minutes of inept finishing and sloppy play in a desperate goalless draw against rock‑bottom Wolves, resulting in boos and insults aimed at Sean Dyche at full time, which sealed a third head coach sacking of the season. Thirty-five shots came and went for Forest, few testing José Sá but the patience of the crowd was thoroughly tested, ultimately costing Dyche his job. It was a missed opportunity for the hosts, who moved only one point further north of 18th-placed West Ham, when three were there for the taking. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest sack manager Dyche after 114 days
items.summary: Nottingham Forest sack Sean Dyche as head coach after just 114 days in charge and are looking for their fourth boss of the season.

items.title: Haaland gives Man City injury scare after HT sub
items.summary: Erling Haaland has given Manchester City an injury scare after Pep Guardiola revealed the striker asked to be substituted during the 3-0 win over Fulham.

items.title: UK PM demands apology from 'offensive' Ratcliffe
items.summary: Several Manchester United supporters groups condemned comments made by co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe after the British billionaire said the U.K. has been "colonized" by immigrants.

items.title: Liverpool's Endo (ankle) set to miss a 'long time'
items.summary: Arne Slot has said midfielder Wataru Endo could be out "for a long time" after picking up a "serious" ankle injury in Liverpool's 1-0 win over Sunderland.

items.title: Miami friendly in PR postponed after Messi injury
items.summary: Inter Miami CF's friendly with Independiente del Valle in Puerto Rico has been postponed for two weeks after Lionel Messi sustained a muscle strain in his left hamstring during its last preseason game against Barcelona SC on Saturday.

items.title: Late arrival & incredible comeback - Burnley deepen Palace woes
items.summary: The Clarets' remarkable first-half fightback keeps alive their slim hopes of Premier League survival, but where does it leave Crystal Palace?

items.title: Man City 'breathing down Arsenal's neck' as title 'belief' grows
items.summary: Pep Guardiola vowed his Manchester City side would "breathe down the neck of Arsenal" as they aim to hunt down the Premier League leaders in this fascinating title race.

items.title: Geen zorgen bij Schreuder na onverwachte nederlaag NEC
items.summary: Dick Schreuder maakt zich allesbehalve zorgen na de verrassende nederlaag van NEC tegen FC Utrecht. Na maanden van euforie en positiviteit diende de ploeg van Ron Jans de Nijmegenaren de eerste nederlaag sinds 2 november 2025 toe. Ook toen was Utrecht overigens de tegenstander.

items.title: Slot telt zegeningen na eerste uitzege in 2026 en heeft slecht nieuws over Endo
items.summary: Grote opluchting bij Arne Slot na de winst van Liverpool op Sunderland. Zijn ploeg verkeert niet in de beste fase, maar de nederlaag tegen Manchester City is in ieder geval wel weggepoetst.

items.title: Van Dijk kopt zich geschiedenisboeken van Liverpool in: 'Een mooie stap voorwaarts
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk heeft woensdagavond een plek in de geschiedenisboeken van Liverpool veroverd. Door te scoren tegen Sunderland, werd de 34-jarige Nederlander niet alleen matchwinner, maar maakte hij bovenal zijn 23ste Premier League-doelpunt in dienst van The Reds. Daarmee is Van Dijk nu de meest scorende verdediger aller tijden van Liverpool.

items.title: Euforie bij FC Utrecht: 'Begon bijna lachwekkend te worden'
items.summary: Er werd woensdag eindelijk weer eens gelachen bij FC Utrecht. De ploeg van trainer Ron Jans won verrassend op bezoek bij nummer drie NEC: 1-3. Het was de eerste zege in de Eredivisie sinds 9 november 2025, toen Ajax werd verslagen.

items.title: Engelse media zien Van Dijk opstaan: 'Dat verdient lof'
items.summary: Engelse media zijn woensdagavond onder de indruk van Liverpool, dat op bezoek bij Sunderland een nipte zege boekte. De ploeg van Arne Slot overtuigde weer eens en dat oogst lof van de pers.

items.title: Virgil van Dijk rises to occasion as Liverpool end Sunderland record
items.summary: Arne Slot does not believe his job security hinges on Liverpool securing Champions League qualification this spring but the Dutchman would much prefer not to put that particular notion to the test. On this evidence there seems a decent chance last season’s title winning manager will not have to. In finally ending Sunderland’s proud unbeaten home record in the Premier League, sixth placed Liverpool left themselves only three points adrift Manchester United and two behind Chelsea. Continue reading...

items.title: Milner reaches milestone but Brighton’s misery goes on after late Villa strike
items.summary: On a night when James Milner made history, equalling his former teammate Gareth Barry’s Premier League appearance record, his former club Aston Villa created more unwanted headlines for Fabian Hürzeler. A Tyrone Mings header, deflected in off Jack Hinshelwood with four minutes of the 90 to play, was sufficient to defeat Brighton and extend their miserable run. Continue reading...

items.title: Manchester City make quick work of Fulham but Haaland off early with ‘niggles’
items.summary: Manchester City’s week is ­moving along sweetly, this win closing the gap to three points to Arsenal, who travel to Brentford on Thursday. On ­Sunday, they defeated Liverpool at Anfield, on Wednesday they downed ­Fulham at home to reel off a 20th consecutive victory against them. The rosiest moment for the title challengers was Erling Haaland’s 39th‑minute strike, a first in the competition from open play in nine games, though more con­cerning was his removal at the break, when City led 3-0. Continue reading...

items.title: Grimsby on cusp of play-off spots after beating Accrington
items.summary: Grimsby brought themselves back to within a point of the Sky Bet League Two play-offs with a 1-0 win over Accrington at Blundell Park.

items.title: Holman stars as Swindon go second with win over Newport
items.summary: Substitute Fletcher Holman's stunning cameo put Swindon into second in League Two as he fired in the opener and set up the own-goal clincher in a 2-0 win over Newport.

items.title: De Zerbi, Heitinga and Pochettino the leading contenders after Tottenham sack Frank
items.summary: Heitinga could be interim option to end of season De Zerbi available after leaving Marseille Tottenham are exploring their options after sacking Thomas Frank on Wednesday morning, with Roberto De Zerbi and one of their current coaches, John Heitinga, in the conversation. It is unclear whether the chief executive, Vinai Venkatesham, and the sporting director, Johan Lange, who are overseeing the process, will favour an interim choice which could be Heitinga. If they did, it would buy them time until the summer when other candidates could be available, including Mauricio ­Pochettino and Andoni Iraola. Continue reading...
